title: Speedboat Rental in Fuengirola Marina: Costs and Tips 2026
html:
Speedboat rental in Fuengirola Marina puts the Costa del Sol within easy reach, with most boats leaving from the Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola and day prices starting near EUR 300 for a small motorboat. BoatHire24 lists self-drive and crewed speedboats here, so families and groups can compare options and book quickly.

Where to set off in Fuengirola Marina

Nearly every speedboat trip begins at the Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola, the town's main marina just below the Sohail Castle. From there the coastline opens up in both directions.

  • Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola: the central departure point, with fuel, parking and a ramped quay.
  • Los Boliches and Carvajal beaches: calm swimming bays just east of the harbour mouth.
  • Castillo Sohail: a landmark fortress you pass on the way out.
  • Benalmadena (Puerto Marina): roughly six nautical miles north-east, with its own harbour.
  • Mijas coast and Calahonda: quieter coves to the south-west for a swim.

Which boat to rent in Fuengirola Marina?

Choose a licence-free motorboat for short coastal pottering, or a 28-foot sport boat such as an Axopar when you want range and speed for a full day. The right pick depends on group size, distance and whether anyone aboard holds a licence.

Speedboats here range from compact day boats to powerful sport cruisers. For the run to Benalmadena you can compare the Limpopo, an Axopar 28 that handles open water comfortably.

  1. Licence-free day boat best for first-timers near the bay, seats up to 5 guests, from EUR 250.
  2. Small sport motorboat good for a half-day with snorkelling stops, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 300.
  3. Axopar 28 sport boat ideal for longer coastal runs, seats up to 7 guests, from EUR 600.
  4. Mid-size crewed speedboat suits groups wanting a skipper aboard, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 700.
  5. RIB tender nimble for beach-hopping and watersports, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 280.

How does booking a speedboat actually work here?

You pick your dates, choose self-drive or crewed, and confirm online, then the operator handles the handover and a short safety briefing at the quay. Most listings show whether fuel is included or charged on return.

The guide on how boat rental works walks through deposits, fuel policies and what to bring. Arrive about 30 minutes early with photo identification for the deposit.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Fuengirola Marina?

Prices vary with boat size, season and whether a skipper is included, but the bands below give a realistic 2026 picture. To see live rates you can browse boats for your dates and filter by length and capacity.

  • A shared boat tour along the Fuengirola coast costs from EUR 35 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day starts around EUR 300 in the marina.
  • A larger speedboat or catamaran for a full day runs from EUR 600 to EUR 900.
  • A crewed speedboat or yacht with a skipper starts near EUR 700 per day.

When is the best season to rent a speedboat in Fuengirola?

The high season runs from June to September, when the sea is warm and settled, and the main local wind to watch is the easterly Levante, which can push up a short swell. Spring and early autumn stay pleasant and cheaper.

Local tip: in summer the Levante usually builds through the afternoon, so book a morning slot for the calmest water and the smoothest ride towards Benalmadena. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Fuengirola Marina or list your own.

On licensing, Spain allows certain low-powered boats to be hired without a formal licence, while larger speedboats require a recognised qualification or an included skipper. The operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat before you book.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 250 for a small boat to EUR 900 for a full-day speedboat
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 3 hours to a full day
  • Capacity: 5 to 8 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Departs: Puerto Deportivo de Fuengirola

Questions about boat rental in Fuengirola Marina

Do I need a licence to rent a speedboat in Fuengirola?

For some smaller, lower-powered boats no formal licence is required in Spain, but most genuine speedboats need a recognised qualification or a hired skipper, and the operator confirms the exact rule for each listing.

How many people fit on a speedboat here?

Most speedboats in Fuengirola Marina carry between 5 and 8 guests, depending on the hull and safety certificate, so confirm the maximum on the listing before booking a larger group.

How far can I cruise in a day?

From Fuengirola you can comfortably reach Benalmadena, around six nautical miles north-east, or quieter Mijas coves to the south-west, with plenty of time to anchor, swim and return before dusk.

Is fuel included in the price?

Fuel policies vary between boats, with some listings including a tank and others charging on return, so read the fuel terms shown alongside the day rate before you confirm.

Speedboat rental in Fuengirola Marina rewards an early start, so compare hulls, confirm the licence rule and check availability before the summer slots fill. Boat owners along the Costa del Sol are also welcome to list their craft and reach renters worldwide.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fuengirola Marina and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
